folio.label: 196r
folio.l: ñet se men wel , by reson doutelees
That ydelnesse is roten , slogardrye
Of whicú æäe neuäe cometú , no good ne encrees
And syn that slouthe , hire holdetú in a lees
Oonly for to slepe , and ete and drynke
And to deuouren , al æ othere swynke
¢ And for to putte vs , from swicú ydelnesse

note.notetxt: 22 Indication in the left margin for a paragraph mark to be included.Est

folio.l: That cause is , of so gret confusiou~
I haue heere doon , my feitúful bysynesse
After the legende , in translaciou~
Rigút of thy glorious lyf , and passiou~
Thow w thy gerland , wrougút of Rose & lilie
The mene I , mayde & martir , Seynt Cecilie
¢ And æè that flour , of virgines art alle

note.notetxt: 29Indication in the left margin for a paragraph mark to be included.MP

folio.l: Of whom æ Bernard , list so wel to wryte
To the , at my bygynnyng , I first calle
Thow confort of vs wrecches , do me endite
Thy maydenes detú , æ wan thurgú hire merite
The eternal lyf , and ouä the fend victorie
As man may after reden , in hire storie
¢ Thow mayde & moder , dougúter of thy Sone

note.notetxt: 36Indication in the left margin for a paragraph mark to be included.MP

folio.l: Thow welle of´ mäcy , synful soules cure
In whom , æ god of bountee chees to wone
Thow humble & heygú , ouer euäy creature
Thow nobledest so ferfortú , oure nature
That no disdeyn , the makere hadde of kynde
His sone in blood & flessú , to clothe & wynde
¢ W Inne the cloistre blisful , of thy sydes

note.notetxt: 43 Indication in the left margin for a paragraph mark to be included.Est

folio.l: Took ma~nes shap~ , the eternal loue & pees
That of the Tryne compas , lord and gyde is
Whom erthe and See , & heuene , out of relees
Ay heryen , and æè virgine wemmelees
Baar of thy body , & dwelledest mayde pure
The creatour , of euäy creature
¢ Assembled is , in the magnificence

note.notetxt: 50Indication in the left margin for a paragraph mark to be included.MP

folio.l: Witú mäcy , goodnesse , & swicú pitee
That æè æat art the Sonne , of excellence
Nat oonly helpest hem , æ preyen the
But ofte tyme , of thy benignytee
Ful freely , er that men , thyn helpe byseche
Thow goost biforn , and art here lyues leche
¢ Now helpe , æè meke & blisful , faire mayde

note.notetxt: 57Indication in the left margin for a paragraph mark to be included.MP
